mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, signals a healthy and expanding economy, which boosts consumer confidence and spending. This translates to higher corporate earnings and, consequently, positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth for 3 consecutive months, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at historically low levels, it signals an accommodative monetary policy stance by the central bank. This encourages investment and risk-taking, supporting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months, but SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of those periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with a low unemployment rate indicate strong consumer demand and purchasing power. This leads to increased corporate revenues and profits across various sectors, which collectively drives the S&P 500 higher.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and UNRATE conditions are met, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, corporate earnings are expected to rise due to increased output, capacity utilization, and efficiency. This positive earnings outlook leads to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns, especially during economic expansions.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth meets the trigger during an expansionary regime, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in housing starts indicates growing confidence in the economy and future demand. This stimulates related industries like construction and finance, contributing to overall economic growth and supporting equity markets.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by more than 50k units on average over 3 months, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such occurrences over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index, erodes purchasing power and can prompt central banks to tighten monetary policy. This increases discount rates and negatively impacts corporate earnings, thus driving S&P 500 returns lower.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 3.0% for 6 consecutive months, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of those periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits. This fundamental deterioration puts downward pressure on S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over 3 months, but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust Gross Domestic Product growth indicates a strong overall economy with increasing aggregate demand. This typically translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, which in turn supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive quarters, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of those periods over a 3-year rolling window.
